segments.text: Yahweh’s word which came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning the nations.

Of Egypt: concerning the army of Pharaoh Necoh king of Egypt, which was by the river Euphrates in Carchemish, which Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon struck in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah.
Prepare the buckler and shield, and draw near to battle!
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Harness the horses, and get up, you horsemen, and st and up with your helmets. Polish the spears, put on the coats of mail.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text:
Why have I seen it? They are dismayed and are turned backward. Their mighty ones are beaten down, have fled in haste, 
and don’t look back. Terror is on every side,
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: says Yahweh.
Don’t let the swift flee away, nor the mighty man escape. In the north by the river Euphratesthey have stumbled and fallen.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: “Who is this who rises up like the Nile, like rivers whose waters surge?
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Egypt rises up like the Nile, like rivers whose waters surge. He says, ‘I will rise up. I will cover the earth. I will destroy cities and its inhabitants.’
segments.speaker: Egypt

segments.text:
Go up, you horses! Rage, you chariots! Let the mighty men go out: Cush and Put, who handle the shield; 
and the Ludim, who handle and bend the bow.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah
The LORD

segments.text:
Go up into Gilead, and take balm, virgin daughter of Egypt. You use many medicines in vain. 
There is no healing for you.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah
Yahweh

segments.text: The word that Yahweh spoke to Jeremiah the prophet, how that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon should come and strike the land of Egypt:

Declare in Egypt, publish in Migdol, 
and publish in Memphis and in Tahpanhes; say, ‘St and up, and prepare, for the sword has devoured around you.’
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text: Why are your strong ones swept away? They didn’t st and, because Yahweh pushed them.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
He made many to stumble. Yes, they fell on one another. They said, ‘Arise! Let’s go again to our own people, and to the land of our birth, 
from the oppressing sword.’
segments.speaker: Soldiers

segments.text: They cried there, ‘Pharaoh king of Egypt is but a noise; he has let the appointed time pass by.’
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: “As I live,
segments.speaker: The King (The LORD of hosts)

segments.text: says the King, whose name is Yahweh of Armies,

surely like Tabor among the mountains, and like Carmel by the sea, 
so he will come.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
You daughter who dwells in Egypt, furnish yourself to go into captivity; for Memphis will become a desolation, and will be burned up, 
without inhabitant.
segments.speaker: Jeremiah

segments.text:
“Egypt is a very beautiful heifer; but destruction out of the north has come. 
It has come.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Also her hired men in the middle of her are like calves of the stall, for they also are turned back. 
They have fled away together. They didn’t st and, for the day of their calamity has come on them, 
the time of their visitation.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Its sound will go like the serpent, for they will march with an army, 
and come against her with axes, as wood cutters.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will cut down her forest,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh,
though it can’t be searched; because they are more than the locusts, and are innumerable.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: The daughter of Egypt will be disappointed; she will be delivered into the hand of the people of the north.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says:
Behold, I will punish Amon of No, and Pharaoh, and Egypt, with her gods and her kings, even Pharaoh, and those who trust in him.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: I will deliver them into the hand of those who seek their lives, and into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of his servants. Afterwards it will be inhabited, as in the days of old,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh.

But don’t you be afraid, Jacob my servant. Don’t be dismayed, Israel; for, behold, I will save you from afar, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Jacob will return, and will be quiet and at ease. 
No one will make him afraid.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Don’t be afraid, O Jacob my servant,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh,

segments.speaker: Yahweh
